title: GCP Security Review — RDF API Project
summary: 14 findings across CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM severity. 4 already fixed in code. 10 require infrastructure changes via gcloud CLI.

findings.title: Default Compute Engine SA has roles/editor
findings.description: The default compute service account (448201086881-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com) has roles/editor, granting project-wide write access to nearly all GCP resources. If any service (Cloud Run, batch VM) is compromised, the attacker inherits full project write permissions.

steps.description: Create a dedicated Cloud Run service account with least-privilege roles
commands: gcloud iam service-accounts create rdf-api-sa --display-name='RDF API Cloud Run'

steps.description: Create a dedicated batch VM service account with least-privilege roles
commands: gcloud iam service-accounts create rdf-batch-sa --display-name='RDF Batch VM'

steps.description: Attach dedicated SA to Cloud Run
commands: gcloud run services update rdf-api --region europe-central2 --service-account=rdf-api-sa@rdf-api-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com

steps.description: Attach dedicated SA to batch VM (requires VM stop/start)
Remove roles/editor from the default compute SA

remediation.rollback: Re-add roles/editor to default SA if services break: gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding rdf-api-project --member='serviceAccount:448201086881-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com' --role='roles/editor'
verification: gcloud run services describe rdf-api --region europe-central2 --format='value(spec.template.spec.serviceAccountName)'

findings.title: Unauthenticated mutation endpoints exposed to the internet
findings.description: POST endpoints for ETL ingestion and job management (sync trigger, scan trigger/stop/reset-cursor) had no authentication guards. Anyone on the internet could trigger ETL runs, start/stop scanners, or reset the scan cursor.

files_changed: app/routers/jobs/routes.py — added CurrentUser + require_admin to all POST endpoints
tests/api/test_jobs_routes.py — updated all tests with admin JWT auth
tests/services/test_code_review_fixes.py — updated ETL tests with admin JWT auth

findings.title: Docker container runs as root
findings.description: The main Dockerfile had no USER directive. The FastAPI application ran as root inside the container, meaning a container escape or RCE would grant root privileges.

files_changed: Dockerfile — added 'useradd appuser' and 'USER appuser' directive

findings.title: Internal upstream URLs leaked in 502 error responses
findings.description: The upstream_error_handler and upstream_request_error_handler in main.py returned upstream_url and error_type fields to clients, exposing internal service topology and implementation details.

files_changed: app/main.py — stripped upstream_url and error_type from 502 JSON responses
tests/api/test_endpoints.py — updated test to verify fields are NOT present

findings.title: Cloud SQL instance has a public IP
findings.description: Cloud SQL instance rdf-postgres has public IP 34.118.73.120 reachable from the internet. The batch VM connects via this public IP with a password in a plaintext .env file. Although authorized networks may restrict access, the public IP is an unnecessary attack surface.
affected_resources: Batch VM .env: DATABASE_URL contains password in plaintext

steps.description: Install Cloud SQL Auth Proxy on the batch VM
Create a systemd service for Cloud SQL Auth Proxy on the batch VM
commands:
cat > /tmp/cloud-sql-proxy.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Cloud SQL Auth Proxy
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=worker
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/cloud-sql-proxy rdf-api-project:europe-central2:rdf-postgres --port 5432
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo cp /tmp/cloud-sql-proxy.service /etc/systemd/system/ && sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable --now cloud-sql-proxy'

steps.description: Update batch VM .env to use localhost instead of public IP
Remove the public IP from Cloud SQL after verifying proxy works

remediation.rollback: Re-enable public IP: gcloud sql instances patch rdf-postgres --assign-ip
verification: gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'sudo systemctl status cloud-sql-proxy'
gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a -- 'PGPASSWORD=<pw> psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U postgres -d rdf -c "SELECT 1"'

findings.title: CORS set to wildcard in production
findings.description: CORS_ORIGINS is set to ["*"] on the production Cloud Run service, allowing any website to make credentialed cross-origin requests to the API. This enables CSRF-style attacks where a malicious site can make API calls using a victim's JWT token.

steps.description: Set CORS_ORIGINS to your actual frontend domains only
commands: gcloud run services update rdf-api --region europe-central2 --set-env-vars 'CORS_ORIGINS=["https://joy-forge-express.lovable.app","https://aaed77c9-b864-4814-8747-ad97402d1f70.lovableproject.com"]'

findings.title: Batch VM has external IP — direct SSH target
findings.description: The batch VM rdf-batch-vm has external IP 34.116.141.233, making it directly reachable from the internet. SSH brute-force attacks are a constant risk. IAP tunneling provides the same access without exposing a public IP.

steps.description: Enable IAP for SSH access
Create a firewall rule allowing IAP TCP forwarding
Grant IAP tunnel user role to your account
Remove the external IP from the VM
commands: gcloud compute instances delete-access-config rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a --access-config-name='External NAT'

steps.description: Add Cloud NAT for outbound internet access (batch workers need to reach ms.gov.pl)
commands: gcloud compute routers nats create rdf-nat --router=rdf-router --region=europe-central2 --nat-all-subnet-ip-ranges --auto-allocate-nat-external-ips

remediation.rollback: Re-add external IP: gcloud compute instances add-access-config rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a
remediation.note: After removing external IP, always SSH via: gcloud compute ssh rdf-batch-vm --zone=europe-central2-a --tunnel-through-iap

findings.title: Secrets stored as plaintext .env on batch VM
findings.description: NordVPN credentials and the database password are stored in plaintext in /opt/rdf-api-project/.env on the batch VM. Any user or process with file read access can extract them.

steps.description: Update systemd services to fetch secrets from Secret Manager at startup instead of reading from .env
steps.note: Modify the ExecStart in krs-scanner.service and rdf-worker.service to wrap the command with a script that fetches secrets: export DATABASE_URL=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=database-url) etc.

steps.description: Create a wrapper script on the VM that fetches secrets and exec's the worker
Update systemd ExecStart to use the wrapper
steps.note: Change ExecStart in krs-scanner.service to: ExecStart=/opt/rdf-api-project/run-with-secrets.sh /opt/rdf-api-project/.venv/bin/python -m batch.runner

steps.description: Remove secrets from .env on the VM (keep only non-sensitive vars)

remediation.prerequisite: SEC-001 must be completed first (batch VM SA needs roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor)

findings.title: GCS buckets lack public access prevention
findings.description: The GCS buckets rdf-project-documents and rdf-project-data were created without public access prevention. If an IAM policy accidentally grants allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers access, the buckets become publicly readable.

steps.description: Enable public access prevention on both buckets
Enable uniform bucket-level access (disables per-object ACLs)

findings.title: No SSL enforcement on Cloud SQL connections
findings.description: Cloud SQL instance rdf-postgres does not enforce SSL for connections. While Cloud Run uses the built-in Auth Proxy (which provides encryption), the batch VM currently connects via public IP without guaranteed SSL, allowing potential man-in-the-middle interception of database traffic.

steps.description: Require SSL for all Cloud SQL connections

remediation.note: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy connections (Cloud Run) are already encrypted and unaffected by this setting. If the batch VM still uses the public IP before SEC-005 is completed, add sslmode=verify-full to its DATABASE_URL.

findings.title: Batch VM uses overly broad storage-full scope
findings.description: The batch VM was created with --scopes=storage-full, granting full read/write/delete access to ALL GCS buckets in the project. Should be scoped to specific buckets via IAM on a dedicated SA.

remediation.note: Resolved automatically by SEC-001 step 4 — switching to a dedicated SA with bucket-scoped IAM replaces the need for broad scopes.

findings.title: Stale backup cron references non-existent DuckDB file
findings.description: deploy/rdf-backup.cron backs up /data/scraper.duckdb to GCS. The project migrated to PostgreSQL, so this file no longer exists. The cron runs as root and silently fails. Should be updated to back up PostgreSQL or removed.

steps.description: Option A: Remove the stale cron if PostgreSQL backups are handled by Cloud SQL automated backups
steps.note: Cloud SQL automated backups are recommended over manual pg_dump crons

steps.description: Option B: Replace with a PostgreSQL backup cron if you need off-GCP backups
steps.note: Update deploy/rdf-backup.cron to use pg_dump instead of gsutil cp on DuckDB

findings.title: Test dependencies shipped in production Docker image
findings.description: requirements.txt includes pytest, pytest-asyncio, respx, and pandas (~120MB). These increase the attack surface, image size, and build time in the production container.

steps.description: Split requirements into production and development files
steps.note: Create requirements-dev.txt with: pytest, pytest-asyncio, respx. Move pandas to requirements-dev.txt if only used for training data scripts (not the API runtime). Update Dockerfile to use requirements.txt (prod only). Update CI to install both.

findings.title: No Cloud Audit Logs for data access
findings.description: Data Access audit logs are not configured for Cloud SQL or Cloud Storage. Without these, you cannot detect or investigate unauthorized data access after the fact.

steps.description: Enable Data Access audit logs for Cloud SQL and Cloud Storage
steps.note: Edit /tmp/iam-policy.json to add auditConfigs, then apply with gcloud projects set-iam-policy

steps.description: Add audit config JSON block to the IAM policy
Apply the updated IAM policy

remediation.note: Data Access logs increase Cloud Logging costs. Monitor billing after enabling. Consider enabling DATA_WRITE only if cost is a concern.

execution_order.note: All three code fixes ship in a single deployment
Create dedicated SAs, tighten CORS, lock down GCS and Cloud SQL. These are independent and can be applied in any order.

execution_order.phase: Phase 3 — Remove public access (this week, after Phase 2)
execution_order.note: SEC-005 (remove Cloud SQL public IP) and SEC-007 (remove VM external IP) require SEC-001 to be done first. SEC-008 (secrets from Secret Manager) requires SEC-001 step 2. Apply SEC-007 last because it changes SSH access method.
SEC-011 is auto-resolved by SEC-001. SEC-012-014 are lower priority cleanup.
